reason: If debug cores/probes are introduced through HDL/IP, debug configuration, reports/logs, or bitstream/debug metadata, existing records cover the artifact and boundary. Main text should name the subcase because AMD UG908 and Intel Debug Tools make debug artifacts visible in the audited vendor-documentation corpus; the generated tool-run inventory contains no debug artifacts because the controlled designs insert no debug cores.

Security-relevant bitstream-generation options are tool options/configuration unless the actor abuses release/signing authority or exposes key material. Main text should name encryption/signing/security-option examples where configured.

candidate: EDA tool binary, plugin, or container image substitution
reason: This is not a vendor vulnerability claim. The post-refresh vendor-corpus analysis treats it as a tool-environment provenance threat: the EDA executable, plugin, Tcl package, IP generator, or build container can affect generated netlists, reports, or bitstreams and therefore forms a distinct transformation-environment boundary.

candidate: IP packaging metadata or IP-XACT tampering
reason: Packaged IP metadata affects IP identity/intake and source integrity. It should be named as IP package metadata, VLNV, or IP-XACT evidence, but does not need a separate main record.
Waivers/exceptions affect signoff evidence and can be handled through constraints, report tampering, and lineage. Name waiver/exception evidence explicitly.

reason: Stale bitstream relabeling is already a bitstream substitution case, and rollback needs release/provenance and programming-target evidence. Name rollback/downgrade explicitly.
FPGA-specific but not uniformly in the core representative flow. Keep as future/supplemental unless the paper claims partial-reconfiguration coverage.

mapping: SIP-2/RPT-2 if HLS is declared in source scope
reason: Current source scope mentions HDL/IP and sometimes HLS. If HLS remains in the system model, explicitly say HLS is treated as source/IP input; otherwise remove HLS from broad claims.

reason: Operationally relevant to EDA but less artifact-centered than timing/resource closure or route-resource exhaustion.

Changing the project part, device family, speed grade, package, or board/pin settings builds the design under wrong target assumptions. The generated corpus exposes build-target identity only inside settings files already mapped CFG-1;CFG-2;CFG-3 (Quartus QSF FAMILY/DEVICE assignments, Vivado XPR Part option; the XPR BoardPart field is empty, so no board-revision artifact exists in the corpus), carried by the option snapshot and target-device record; spoofing the physical programming target at deployment remains BSD-4.

Simulation-only constructs (translate_off/translate_on regions, synthesis pragmas, simulation-only or black-box models) enter as HDL/IP source changes (SIP-2) or verification-collateral changes (SIM-1); synthesis logs and netlist lineage are already carried by TRN-1/RPT-2 mappings. The distinguishing equivalence-check requirement is absent from the evaluated corpus (the flows run no formal equivalence checking), so the candidate fails the reconciliation test as a separate record; equivalence-check evidence is a scope extension.
